variants.title: SCEMQA 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: Young doctors in emergency-room training were randomly assigned to two groups and given the same description of common heart-disease symptoms. Group A was told the patient was a 55-year-old female, and Group B was told the patient was a 55-year-old male; all other patient characteristics were identical. If 70% of doctors in the female-patient group ordered cardiac tests and 85% of doctors in the male-patient group ordered cardiac tests, write the appropriate test statistic to test whether doctors are less likely to order cardiac tests when the patient is female.

rationale: Converted the multiple-choice calculus question into an equivalent open-ended question while preserving the graph reference and expected numeric answer.

Based on this distribution, what measure should be used to describe the center of the distribution?
